message:
refactor(server,db,core): async supervisor architecture, SQL query caching, MVCC async backoff, identity cache optimization

Server (lib.rs, tui_app.rs, tui_bridge.rs):
- Migrate from thread-based to async supervisor architecture using
  `spawn_http_supervisor_task()` with RuntimeHandle instead of dedicated
  OS threads; add `build_http_runtime()`, async `probe_http_healthz()`
  using HttpClient, `PanicAsIoFuture` wrapper for panic-safe task
  monitoring, `recv_http_supervisor_result()` for clean shutdown
- Replace `mpsc::channel` with `asupersync::channel::mpsc` for unified
  async channel infrastructure; TUI mode uses shared runtime

Database (queries.rs):
- Convert SQL query construction caches from returning `String` to
  returning `&'static str` via OnceLock, eliminating per-query string
  allocation for approved_contact_sql, recent_contact_union_sql, and
  list_message_recipient_names queries
- Consolidate `get_agent_last_mail_activity` from 2 separate queries
  (sent + read/ack) into a single UNION ALL query reducing round-trips
- Convert `mvcc_backoff()` from blocking `thread::sleep` to async
  `asupersync::time::sleep` to avoid blocking the async runtime during
  MVCC retry backoff

Search (search_two_tier.rs):
- Fix fast-embedding fallback logic: when fast embed fails, fall through
  to quality refinement if quality_embedder is available regardless of
  quality_only flag, returning None only when no fallback exists

Identity (identity.rs):
- Optimize resolve_path_cache lookups using Cow<str> to avoid allocating
  a String for cache key lookups that miss; only allocate on insertion

Robot (robot.rs): Expand thread subject query to include created_ts and
  id columns for richer thread metadata
Threads (threads.rs): Minor thread display fix
Reservation index: Minor cleanup

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1950,7 +1950,7 @@ fn load_thread_subject(
     let rows = conn
         .query_sync(
             &format!(
-                "SELECT m.subject
+                "SELECT m.subject, m.created_ts, m.id
                  FROM messages m
                  WHERE {thread_where}
                  {order_clause}
